title: Fix bridge-zephyr duplicate LICHEN source linkage under west workspace
description: Discovered while validating project-LICHEN-2067.1. firmware/bridge-zephyr now configures build-epoch metadata correctly, but a qemu_x86 build in the current west workspace fails at link because the app directly compiles firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/{crash_info,lichen_util,ipv6_addr}.c while the modern LICHEN module also links lichen/subsys/lichen/l2 objects with the same symbols. Evidence command on EC2 i-04d7dc29208da05dd: SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1717777777 west build -p always -b qemu_x86 -d build/bridge_source_epoch_nomodule firmware/bridge-zephyr; configure succeeded and .config had CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX=1717777777 and CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=y, then link failed with multiple definitions for crash_info_store, log_const_crash_info, lichen_sha256, lichen_eui64_to_iid, lichen_make_link_local, and related IPv6 helpers. Decide whether bridge-zephyr should stop vendoring those sources, disable the modern module sources for this app, or retire the legacy bridge entrypoint.
notes: Implementation: bridge-zephyr now compiles only src/main.c and consumes module-owned lichen/subsys/lichen/l2 headers/sources; removed bridge legacy subsys/Kconfig import so module Kconfig owns CONFIG_LICHEN*; restored shared module behavior by making CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2 select LICHEN_LINK; fixed qemu/native main.c unused ret warning; added python/tests/firmware/test_bridge_zephyr_cmake.py with static ownership checks plus opt-in LICHEN_RUN_ZEPHYR_SMOKE qemu_x86 and native_sim LoRa-loopback builds. Reviews: initial review found P1 that removing legacy Kconfig could drop LICHEN_LINK for LoRa builds; fixed by select LICHEN_LINK in l2/Kconfig. Final review P2s found text-only tests and bridge board config filename mismatch; added gated Zephyr smoke tests and created child project-LICHEN-2067.1.1.1 for board config filename audit. Local checks: python3 -m pytest -q python/tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py python/tests/firmware/test_bridge_zephyr_cmake.py -> 8 passed, 2 skipped (Zephyr smokes gated); cd python && python3 -m ruff check tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py tests/firmware/test_bridge_zephyr_cmake.py -> all checks passed; git diff --check -> clean. EC2 i-0099fd91458caba77 in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted; cache worktree dirty/behind so preserved it and used clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-2067-1-1-validate at origin/main fa32544 plus patch. EC2 pytest: LICHEN_RUN_ZEPHYR_SMOKE=1 python3 -m pytest -q python/tests/firmware/test_bridge_zephyr_cmake.py -> 5 passed, 1 pytest config warning for unknown asyncio_mode. EC2 qemu build: SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1717777777 west build -p always -b qemu_x86 -d build/bridge_qemu_source_epoch firmware/bridge-zephyr -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-2067-1-1-validate/lichen -> passed, no warnings/errors, CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX=1717777777, SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH source, CONFIG_LICHEN_IPV6=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK not set as expected without LoRa, module-owned l2 common sources only, RAM 166480 B / 31 MB (0.51%). EC2 nucleo_wl55jc no-flash compile: SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1717777777 west build -p always -b nucleo_wl55jc -d build/bridge_nucleo_wl55jc firmware/bridge-zephyr -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-2067-1-1-validate/lichen -> passed, no warnings/errors, module-owned l2 common sources only, FLASH 57152 B / 256 KB (21.80%), RAM 16212 B / 64 KB (24.74%); plain board did not enable CONFIG_LORA, tracked board config filename coverage gap in project-LICHEN-2067.1.1.1.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware scope: bridge-zephyr no longer compiles legacy vendored LICHEN sources or Kconfig under a west workspace; module-owned L2/Kconfig is the single owner, LICHEN_LORA_L2 selects LICHEN_LINK, qemu/native_sim smoke coverage added, EC2 qemu/native_sim/nucleo validation passed. Remaining bridge board config filename audit is tracked in child project-LICHEN-2067.1.1.1.
